Protein names: Biotin biosynthesis bifunctional protein BioCD [Includes: Malonyl-[acyl-carrier protein] O-methyltransferase (Malonyl-ACP O-methyltransferase) (EC 2.1.1.197) (Biotin synthesis protein BioC); ATP-dependent dethiobiotin synthetase BioD (EC 6.3.3.3) (DTB synthetase) (DTBS) (Dethiobiotin synthase)]
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: In the N-terminal section; belongs to the methyltransferase superfamily. {ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:P12999}.; SIMILARITY: In the C-terminal section; belongs to the dethiobiotin synthetase family. {ECO:0000255|HAMAP-Rule:MF_00336}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=(7R,8S)-7,8-diammoniononanoate + CO2 + ATP = (4R,5S)-dethiobiotin + ADP + phosphate + 3 H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:15805, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:16526, ChEBI:CHEBI:30616, ChEBI:CHEBI:43474, ChEBI:CHEBI:149469, ChEBI:CHEBI:149473, ChEBI:CHEBI:456216; EC=6.3.3.3; Evidence={ECO:0000255|HAMAP-Rule:MF_00336}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=malonyl-[ACP] + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = malonyl-[ACP] methyl ester + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:17105, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:9623, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:9954, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:78449, ChEBI:CHEBI:78845; EC=2.1.1.197; Evidence={ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:P12999};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Converts the free carboxyl group of a malonyl-thioester to its methyl ester by transfer of a methyl group from S-adenosyl-L-methionine (SAM). It allows synthesis of pimeloyl-ACP via the fatty acid synthetic pathway (By similarity). {ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:P12999}.; FUNCTION: Catalyzes a mechanistically unusual reaction, the ATP-dependent insertion of CO2 between the N7 and N8 nitrogen atoms of 7,8-diaminopelargonic acid (DAPA, also called 7,8-diammoniononanoate) to form a ureido ring. {ECO:0000255|HAMAP-Rule:MF_00336}.
